title: LME Aluminium Price Surge Hits $3,269/Tonne in March 2026
summary: LME aluminium prices reached $3,269 per tonne amid inventory drawdowns and supply constraints. The surge reflects ongoing market tightness in early 2026. This development highlights fundamental pressures on the aluminium market.

title: Aluminium Prices Soar as Tariffs and Energy Costs Reshape the Market
summary: Aluminium prices hit near four-year highs due to tariffs, high energy costs, and strong demand, benefiting US producers like Alcoa and Century Aluminum. Goldman Sachs raised its H1 2026 forecast to $3,150/ton, while US premiums exceeded $1/pound. Downstream sectors face higher costs and supply risks.

title: Al price forecasts hit $4,000/t on Middle East conflict
summary: LME three-month aluminium prices reached $3,372/t due to Middle East conflict disrupting supplies via the Strait of Hormuz. Forecasts now predict prices above $4,000/t amid production stoppages by Qatalum, Alba, and others. Regional premiums in Europe and the US are surging on tight supply.

title: Aluminum prices on March 12, 2026, approached a four-year high
summary: LME three-month aluminium prices rose 1.32% to $3,502.50 per ton, nearing a four-year high. The increase reflects persistent bullish trends in the market. Global supply dynamics continue to drive upward pressure.

title: After US tariffs, aluminium price surge due to Middle East conflict causing concern to Coca-Cola
summary: Aluminium prices surged to a four-year high near $3,500 per tonne in March 2026, exacerbated by US tariffs and Middle East conflict. This has raised concerns for major consumers like Coca-Cola. The combination of factors is tightening global supply.

title: Aluminum prices in March 2026
summary: Aluminum prices have surged over 22% year-to-date on LME, outpacing gold amid Middle East geopolitical tensions. The Strait of Hormuz, handling 20% of non-China aluminium supply, is a key disruption point. This industrial metal is behaving like a safe-haven asset.

title: The 2026 Aluminum Crisis: Hormuz Blockade Halts Gulf Swing Smelters and prices skyrocketing
summary: A Hormuz blockade has halted aluminum production at Gulf smelters, stranding 6 million tons of supply and redrawing the global production map. Chinese producers benefit from diverted cheap raw materials while costs plummet for them amid skyrocketing global prices. Responses include proposals for the first new US aluminum smelter in 50 years and tariffs on imports.

title: A MOMENT AGO: Trump Stunned as U.S. Aluminum Supply Hits Zero
summary: US aluminum supply faces crisis due to declining domestic smelting capacity covering less than 10% of demand, exacerbated by Middle East disruptions where alumina reserves last only weeks. Middle Eastern smelters like Catalum and Alba are scaling back production amid raw material shortages, with restarts taking 6-12 months. US tariffs aim to boost domestic output but offer no immediate relief.

title: How the Iran war is squeezing metals markets and key industries
summary: The Iran war disrupts Middle East aluminum supply, which provides 21% of unwrought and 13% of wrought imports, pushing prices to 4-year highs. Producers like Aluminium Bahrain and others face raw material shortages, with smelter restarts requiring 12 months. Chinese control over global aluminum prices may limit sustained gains.

title: Aluminum Prices Rise Amid Iran War
summary: Aluminum Bahrain, the world's largest smelter, has cut production by 19% due to the ongoing Middle East war. CRU Group notes rising prices from these disruptions. The conflict continues to impact global aluminum supply chains.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026: Pioneering Sustainability and Innovation
summary: The global aluminum industry will gather at ALUMINIUM 2026 in Düsseldorf, Germany, from October 6–8, focusing on sustainability, innovation, and collaboration. The event highlights secondary aluminum's role in decarbonization and advancements in metal processing, recycling, casting, automation, and surface finishing. New features include Hall 7A for semi-finished products and the Sustainability & Recycling Conference.

title: Aluminium Giants Hit Major Milestone with Low-Carbon Production
summary: Elysis, a joint venture of Alcoa and Rio Tinto, has installed its carbon-free inert anode technology in a full-scale 450-kA cell, a major breakthrough for low-carbon aluminum production. This scales up from their 100-kA pilot used in products like Apple devices and Audi wheels, aiming to replace the 140-year-old smelting process. The technology can be integrated into existing or new smelters by the end of the decade.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026: Driving Innovation for a Sustainable Future
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 emphasizes sustainability, particularly secondary aluminum for decarbonization, alongside topics like metal processing, recycling, and applications in mobility and construction. Supported by Aluminium Deutschland and European Aluminium, it features themed pavilions and serves as a platform for innovation and collaboration. Event director Patrick Herman underscores its role in addressing challenges and showcasing future potential.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,148 CNY/T on March 20, 2026, up 0.48% from the previous day, with rebar futures steady around CNY 3,140 per tonne near seven-week highs. Chinese steel output fell 3.6% to 160 million tons in the first two months due to curtailed production amid weak demand and seasonal slowdowns from Lunar New Year and pollution controls.

title: US flat steel pricing continues climb, lower March scrap could be balanced by low steel imports
summary: Weekly US flat steel prices continued their ascent, with hot-rolled coils up $10/nt amid solid demand. Shredded scrap rose $30/gt, potentially finishing near $395-405/gt, balanced by low steel imports. Other updates include Chinese domestic PPGI prices edging up and global HRC prices firming on rising costs and geopolitical risks.

title: US flat steel prices mostly higher amid solid demand, Mideast war could stall
summary: US flat steel prices were mostly higher due to solid demand, though Mideast war risks could stall momentum. This reflects ongoing weekly price climbs in the US flat steel market.

title: Market Update: March 2026
summary: Domestic steel pricing continues to climb above $1,000, driven by trade restrictions limiting North American supply outside the US. Ongoing factors support upward price trends in the steel market.

title: U.S. Steel Production Up 4.2% Year-on-Year for Week Ending March 14, 2026
summary: U.S. domestic raw steel production reached 1.774 million net tons for the week ending March 14, 2026, with a capacity utilization rate of 76.7%. This represents a 4.2% increase compared to the same week in 2025, though it reflects a marginal 0.9% decline from the prior week. Year-to-date production through March 14, 2026, totaled 18.613 million net tons, marking a 4.9% rise from the same period in 2025.

title: U.S. Weekly Steel Output Jumps 4.2% Year-on-Year: AISI
summary: U.S. mills produced 1.774 million net tons for the week ending March 14, 2026, up 4.2% year-over-year but down 0.9% week-over-week from March 7, 2026. The data indicates the industry has moved beyond last year's trough, with year-to-date production reaching 18.613 million net tons at 77.2% average utilization, a 4.9% gain compared to the same period in 2025.

title: Steel Consuming Market Outlook March 2026
summary: Construction planning is cooling in early 2026 but remains at historically high levels, suggesting slower near-term activity with a solid pipeline for future steel demand. Residential housing construction is rebounding, with January 2026 housing starts up 7.2% from December and 9.5% above a year earlier, driven by multi-family projects that are especially steel intensive.

title: European Steel Industry News - Weekly Podcast
summary: EU steel demand is stabilizing after three years of contraction, though record import penetration and political opposition to carbon costs complicate the recovery. The UK has unveiled a landmark Steel Strategy with aggressive trade measures, while rising prices across rebar and stainless flats signal tightening supply, with mill lead times stretching into late May.

title: Weekly Market Recap (March 21) – Copper Prices Surge Past $11,000
summary: Copper futures on COMEX surged past $11,000 per ton to $11,270 amid US tariff threats under President Trump, while LME prices crossed $10,000 with a widening premium. This has redirected over 100,000 tons of copper to the US, creating regional shortages and bullish sentiment. Persistent raw material shortages and potential smelter cuts are exacerbating supply concerns.

title: Copper Drops $0.19 | Scrap Metal Prices Mixed — March 19, 2026
summary: Copper scrap prices fell $0.19 per pound across major grades on the ScrapMonster Price Index, with COMEX copper settling at $548 per pound (down 4.9%) and LME 3-month at $12,395.50 per ton (down 2.97%). The decline aligns with a stronger US dollar, demand concerns from rising energy costs, and geopolitical disruptions. Traders watch key support levels amid potential further downside.

title: Copper Drops $0.19 | March 19, 2026
summary: Copper scrap prices dropped $0.19 per pound across major grades, marking the biggest market move that day. This reflects broader declines in copper futures due to macroeconomic pressures.

title: Copper Supply Crunch Intensifies: Impact on Global Renewable Energy Sector
summary: China's top copper smelters agreed to cut production by over 10% for 2026 due to collapsed Treatment and Refining Charges, indicating insufficient raw ore availability. Copper prices on the London Metal Exchange have reached a volatile range of $12,000 to $14,500 per metric ton as the global economy faces a structural supply deficit affecting the renewable energy transition.

title: Copper wipes out 2026 gains as iran war punishes metals markets
summary: Copper prices slumped below $12,000 per tonne in London, erasing year-to-date gains as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East negatively impacted metals markets.

title: Mint Innovation, HP Achieve Closed-Loop Copper Breakthrough
summary: Mint Innovation and HP Inc. announced the industry's first batch of certified closed-loop recycled copper produced from electronic waste using Mint's biosorption technology. This milestone enables traceable, high-purity copper recovery from end-of-life printed circuit boards for reuse in HP's PC manufacturing, offering a sustainable alternative to energy-intensive smelting. The process supports circular economy goals and scalability, with Mint building a U.S. facility in Longview, Texas, for 2027 launch.

title: Can Copper Extend Its Gains in 2026? | Presented by CME Group
summary: After a 41% gain in 2025, copper prices rallied above $6 in early 2026, driven by surging demand from AI data centers, EVs, and renewables projected to reach 42 million metric tons by 2040. Supply deficits of 330,000 tons are expected in 2026 due to mine disruptions and declining ore grades. U.S. tariff policies under President Trump, including up to 50% levies, are reshaping supply chains and inflating premiums.

title: Nickel fell to 16885 USD/T on March 20, 2026
summary: Nickel prices fell to 16,885 USD/T on March 20, 2026, down 1.05% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have declined 2.31%, though they remain 4.16% higher year-over-year. Nickel futures hit a 4-week low of 16,817 USD/T on March 19 amid global risk aversion from Middle East tensions strengthening the US dollar.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Global nickel prices retreated 1.08% to $16,853.62 per ton amid Middle East tensions inflating costs and strengthening the US dollar. Downside is capped by Indonesia’s ore production quotas and supply risks. Analysts forecast prices to settle in a $15,000-$16,000 range due to ongoing surpluses.

title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Price Rises Toward US$17,420/Ton Level (Tuesday, March 17, 2026)
summary: The 3-month forward nickel price rose 0.49% to US$17,420 per ton on March 17, 2026, with 2.99% year-to-date growth. Weekly growth was 0.2% at an average of US$17,480 per ton. Annually, prices trend downward over five years, but monthly monitoring shows rises over the last 12 months.

title: FLASH: Indonesia's 2026 nickel production estimated at 209 million tonnes, nearly 20% below RKAB quotas
summary: Indonesia's Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources estimates 2026 nickel ore production at 209 million tonnes, about 20% below the RKAB target of 260-270 million tonnes. The reduction aims to increase reserves and stabilize global prices. Actual production has historically been lower than RKAB targets.

title: Nickel Higher on Mine Suspension
summary: Nickel prices rose 1.6% to $16,910, breaking 14-month highs due to the suspension of Vale’s Indonesia mine. The Indonesian government announced plans to cut nickel ore output by a third in 2026 to support prices. This adds to supply concerns amid other global disruptions.

title: Indonesian nickel plants halt output after deadly landslide
summary: Four Chinese-run nickel plants in Indonesia's Morowali Industrial Park halted production following a deadly landslide at a waste site last month. These HPAL facilities account for 30% of Indonesia’s HPAL capacity and are under review. The shutdown highlights scrutiny on waste management in nickel processing amid environmental risks.

title: How nickel mining will be essential for powering the clean energy transition
summary: Nickel mining is crucial for green technologies due to its unique properties in the clean energy transition. The industry is reducing its carbon footprint, with member companies cutting GHG emissions by 9% and committing to further reductions. High-efficiency nickel recycling, at 68%, supports a circular economy by re-entering products like stainless steel and batteries.

title: Nickel Industries showcases sustainability in quarterly update
summary: Nickel Industries reported zero lost-time injuries and 17.7 million safe man-hours in their quarterly update, emphasizing workforce safety as key to sustainable operations. They advanced sustainability through a strategic investment in Excelsior Nickel Cobalt and received awards for ESG leadership. This reflects their focus on safety, governance, and global supply chain standards.

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
summary: The Nickel Institute blog features articles on sustainability, including NOVONIX’s all-dry, zero-waste cathode synthesis technology to cut costs and environmental impact of nickel-based production. Topics cover green hydrogen, EU Critical Raw Materials Act, cellulosic ethanol with nickel alloys, and nickel's sustainability across its value chain. It addresses supply concerns for electric vehicles and cleaner energy.

title: Tenaris to supply line pipe, OCTG, coating and services for Sakarya Phase 3 in offshore Turkey
summary: Tenaris has been awarded a contract to supply line pipe, casing, coating, and services for the third phase of the Sakarya gas field project in Turkey. Seamless pipes are produced at Tenaris’s Dalmine mill in Italy, featuring advanced coatings and technologies. The contract also includes welded pipes from Brazil and is executed under Tenaris’s One Line® service model.

title: Tenaris awarded pipe supply contract for Sakarya gas field phase 3 in Turkey
summary: Luxembourg-headquartered Tenaris has secured a contract for seamless and welded pipes, coating, and services for the Sakarya gas field phase 3 operated by TPAO in Turkey. Seamless pipes for flowlines are produced at the Dalmine mill in Italy, while welded pipes come from the Confab mill in Brazil. The deal includes 22,700 mt of seamless pipes and 87,800 mt of welded pipes for export infrastructure.

title: From production to installation: Tenaris brings integrated solutions to MCE 2026
summary: Tenaris will showcase hot-rolled seamless pipes for civil and industrial applications at Mostra Convegno Expocomfort 2026 in Milan, Italy, from March 24 to 27. The Piombino production facility and service center in Italy support this offering with efficient logistics, coatings, and certifications like Environmental Product Declarations. The company also provides pre-assembly services to enhance project efficiency.
